reviews.review_description:
This game is a combat focused platformer with some interesting mechanics: for instance, the third-person character is not the only one that can attack/interact since your first-person "god" persona can also throw substances to enemies/environment. The graphics are decent and the environments look interesting with a bleak atmosphere that fits well the lore of the game. The game can be pretty hard (definitely the platformer in VR with the hardest combat that I've played), but the combat is good and it has good boss fights.
The big issue with this game is the camera. The camera is positioned at the top and it follows your character from very close proximity which not only gives limited view in lots of situations causing you to die (in a game that's already hard), but worst of all it forces you to be looking down for most of time. Every time I play this game for a while it gives me neck pain because I have to keep looking down and there is no way to position the camera back or down. I've played lots of VR platformers before (Moss I and II, Astrobot, Max Mustard, Trover Saves the Universe, Lucky's Tail, Ven VR Adventure) and I never had this issue with those games. I don't understand why the camera is positioned in such a way since this is not the type of platformer like in Ven VR where you need to traverse the environment by jumping on moving platforms, so there is no reason for the camera to keep following the character from so up close (why not have a distant fixed camera angle that never follows the player like in Moss?). After beating the third boss I decided to give up as playing this game is like putting a book in my lap and keep reading in that position which is very uncomfortable. It's a shame that the camera is in such a bad position as the gameplay is actually good.
Loving the world and character designs. I really enjoy the combat and the 3rd person perspective. :)

I have seen a lot of comparisons to the Moss series which I love but they are really two different games.  Moss is a puzzle platformer focused more on the puzzles and this is more focused on combat.  Both are awesome and should not be compared.  
This game kind of reminds me of Hollow Knight but in VR.  I think any one that enjoys action platformers with lots of combat will definitely enjoy this.  Graphics and audio are good and the mechanics work really well.  If I had one complaint it would be the camera angle in spots.  I find myself looking straight down in spots, especially if you decide to back track to collect something  and I feel they could pan out in them spots so you are not tweaking your neck.  Maybe in a future update this will be addressed.
I enjoy this game a lot and could see a sequel with more added mechanics and upgrades in the future, maybe a metroidvania style game in this world🤞🤞

Am roughly half way through the game and I'm having a blast only bad part is when my oculus dies and I have to wait for it to charge again so I can play more. Super fun combat and throwing mixtures at enemies and the hack and slash play really keeps me engaged. Boss battles are really cool and challenging. 

Edit. I changed my review after completing the game. About half way through the game became pretty repetitive and kind of boring and the combat wasn't fun and the *puzzles were more annoying than a fun challenge. The camera angles at times made me fly off and die after completing a challenging battle forcing me to replay it over and over again. The final boss battle I didn't even complete because it just bothered me and I kept dying and it wasn't a fun challenge more just dashing and hoping you don't get hit. Not a bad game and good parts and a cool concept just overall it didn't deliver as a whole.

There is no option to change character movement from left hand to the right hand. The game looks amazing, but without this, the game is unplayable for me.

Should have a portuguese translation too, i am sad, i wish i could play this.

developer_response.body: Hello Kaycke and thank's for playing Mixture! The option to switch your dominant hand will come in a future update soon. In regards to Portuguese localisation, it's not currently on our list but we will note it as a request :)

reviews.review_description:
The game is amazing. Controlling two heroes works great.
The game will especially appeal to Moss fans, but not only.
Great adventure, I recommend it to everyone.

reviews.review_title: More for fans of Dark Souls than Moss
reviews.review_description:
Update:

Thanks for the quick reply!  I played it some more at lunch. The atmosphere keeps drawing me back in.

The combat is getting more challenging but also more interesting from a strategic standpoint.

I also made it to a more involved platforming section that had a bit of light puzzle solving.

I’m raising my score.

Original Review:

A word of warning for Moss fans who have read the “if you love Moss, you’ll love Mixture” reviews, this is like Moss only in the basic concept where you’re a godlike being guiding someone else through an adventure.

The gameplay is much, much more challenging than Moss.  In battle you guide the character around with the left thumb stick, fight with A and B buttons, gather resources to mix spells from the environment with your left hand, and choose and cast spells with your right.  Doing all this while under attack from all sides and above is far more difficult than anything in Moss except maybe for Moss’s final bosses. There’s no healing by grabbing the character either like you can in Moss. You heal by first casting a spell on an enemy, then if you defeat it before the spell wears off, you’ll regain a bit of health. In my first few hours of gameplay combat has been what I’ve been doing the most. I can’t even imagine how difficult the bosses will become later.  I’ve only encountered one so far, and they unleash that on you at the end of the tutorial level.

Unlike Moss, there doesn’t seem to be any puzzles in the game so far, just basic platforming in between a lot of combat, so if your favorite part of Moss was solving puzzles, you may be disappointed with Mixture.

If your favorite part of Moss was the combat, but you wish it had been more of a challenge, then you should really enjoy Mixture.

The environments, character designs, and animations are well executed, everything runs smoothly, and the controls are precise and responsive.  To lessen the difficulty slightly, (but not by much), you can enable auto aim for spell casting.  The one bug I encountered is that if you enable auto aim from the pause menu within the game it doesn’t target enemies. If you enable it from the main menu it works fine.

If this game had multiple difficulty levels it would easily be a four or five star game that I could recommend to almost anyone. As it is now, it’s a niche game that’s strictly for hardcore gamers.

I sorta like the challenge, but without the puzzle elements to provide a break between the frequent combat sequences after several hours of playing it’s starting to feel repetitive and more like work than play. I’m no longer excited to play it when I put on the headset, but I’m going to keep at it for a while to see if it becomes more interesting.

(Full disclosure, I was given a key to this game.)

developer_response.body:
Hey there Jioxx and thank you for the very complete review. Mixture is indeed a more action oriented game in the likes of hack and slash action adventures, well spotted. 

Also, we are looking at implementing varying levels of difficulty in an upcoming patch as well as some tweaking to the initial onboarding level, we appreciate your feedback.

reviews.review_description: The devs knew that they wanted something unique and fun when making this. The puzzles are great and you get to be an alchemist throwing vials at enemies. If you ever had chemistry class and felt the urge to throw chems around, this is your opportunity!

reviews.review_description: lost interest before beating first boss

developer_response.body: We're sorry you don't like the first minutes of the game, beauregardener. However, we would still encourage you to hold on a little longer. What transpires up until the first boss is just the tutorial, and Mixture opens up quite a bit after that, both in terms of gameplay, story and visuals. In any case, we appreciate your opinion!

reviews.review_description: This game starts off a bit slow a you may think this not for you, but push through and there is a great game here it is like moss but does not match on the graphics which is the only reason it get a 4, but and it’s a big but it make up for it in the co,op Strategy ￼you have to apply when fighting in battle. You can level up your Character ￼and you find new spells for your Alchemist￼. My first play we a solid 2and a half hours play through. There is nice amount of content here a I find the game gets better the more you play it as you unlock more. I feel the game has the hack and slash feel about with a interesting story. I would recommend this game if you liked moss or hack and slash game. Also uses bhaptic which adds to the immersion ￼in this fun world.

At first glance this might seem like a Moss clone but it's much more than that. With great action and puzzles this could be the sleeper VR title of 2023.

It's also got a stunning style and world that draws you into it's adventure. Definitely worth a go, speaking of which... 

Why not give it a try with my 25% off friend referral.

reviews.review_description:
First I'm loving the story, art style, music, but the combat is really getting me sometimes, mostly the Auto aim. I'm at the octopus like boss and alot of the time I'll look at the red crystals throw the vial and it will throw it at some random  enemy even though my view is on the crystals. Also wondering at the same boss, I know to get the boss to swing and hit the crystals you have to be on the front of the platform, but when I stay towards the front on the last crystal he hits the ground twice on the edge of the platform with the tentacles and leaves ground the damages you, then sent up the swing, but he never hits on that side. I tried standing closer to that edge but the damaging ground prevents me. 


Changed my rating to 5 star. Thanks for the quick response by the developer.

developer_response.body:
Hi Hewn! 

Great to hear you've got drawn into Mixture's game world.

For the boss fight - You don't have to trigger the huge tentacle swings with Sola's movement. The boss will swing at her in a pattern.
We'll have a look at the auto-aim logic in this scene. But remember you can always turn the auto-aim off in the Menu Settings. It might  turn out, you'll be more comfortable without it. 

And probably the most helpful tip for this fight, is to throw the Dissolve (Green) mixture on the damaging ground areas.
It will get rid of the substance that hurts you. That makes the fight significantly more manageable.

reviews.review_title: Push over Quill, here comes Sola
reviews.review_description:
Watch my video review on YouTube IronbugVR - Mixture

Mixture is a first person and third person platforming game. It has gameplay similar to the game Moss. You will control a small character named Sola and guide her through the levels. At the same time you will also play in first person as a powerful alchemist helping Sola on her way.

I found this game to be more interactive from the first person view, and the combat much more challenging from the third person view. 

This is a great platformer / first person adventure, that is challenging and has some epic giant boss fights. If you are a fan of Moss and are looking for the next great VR platforming adventure, pickup Mixture. This game will not disappoint.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the comprahensive write-up Ironbug!

reviews.review_description:
I really wanted to like this game. The story and characters give a sense that the team who made it had passion, but the actual game design has a slew of serious flaws, some of which had me quit out several times.
* Visual feedback for Sola's combat is lackluster. Enemies do not give enough feedback when hit so it's easy to wonder if you're swinging out of range or actually hitting. There's not much in the way of appreciable hitsparks, there's no hit-stop whatsoever as far as I can tell, flinch animations can be difficult to discern from dying animations, the enemies themselves don't have a strongly visible flash or anything when hit. Sometimes the health 'dial' around the enemy will also clip through the floor so you're left uncertain if they're flinching or dying until they get back up/dissolve.
* Checkpointing is awful at several parts of the game. There's quite a few that are a decent walk away from something that can kill you, so you're forced to trudge back to the actually dangerous bit, wasting a bunch of time. One particularly bad checkpoint for me though was in idol up: I quit out because I was low on battery and when I returned the checkpoint was ABOVE a monster that blocks access to alchemic resources and you obviously start with none so I had to go back down to kill the mob and any that spawned before climbing back up to the checkpoint to continue from where I left off.
* Randomly dying is a big problem. I've lost count of how many times I've done the jump attack and instead of slamming into the ground I've fallen through the map and died (the most recent memory of it was right in the middle of the raft to the idol up level). There's also a fairly inconsistent fall distance that will kill you in mid-air if you go down too much, even though sola can glide. 
* 'Platforming' can be inconsistent. One bounce I had to deal with in Idol up wasn't high enough the first 3 times but was fine the 4th attempt. What did I do different? nothing as far as I can tell :/

The game is really amusing! Or should I rather say, amazing. You are able to combine ingredients and progress the story using your alchemist mastery. It's literally what I was doing my whole childhood, combining baking powder with water and throwing it away, waiting for explosion.
The art is also a masterpiece. You are able to feel your opponents fear, when you step on their way.
Is there a chance to get a soundtrack on a Spotify?

developer_response.body:
Haha! Thanks for both the baking powder anecdote and the glowing five star review, Lukisio! :D 
We can't promise the sountrack will end up on Spotify, but we'll jot it down on the list to investigate it.
Thanks again!

reviews.review_description: I love Sola as it’s my brothers name. I like the fact that he speaks gibberish and you have to figure out through a comic style text conversation. It’s fun to watch how Sola and Ghost interact with each other as the story unfolds. Great adventure games that may benefit from some graphical fidelity updates in the future. Kudos to the developers

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to play Mixture and dropping a review! 🙏
